title: Dianthus cutandae
intro:

Dianthus caryophyllus, commonly known as Sweet William, is a fragrant, flowering plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as respiratory issues, skin conditions, and menstrual disorders, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Dianthus caryophyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dianthus caryophyllus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
